⚖ Compare formations

Tap up to 5 formations in the grid above, then run the comparison.

Pick a formation above, then confirm to build your 23-slot board.
Custom formation (build your own)

Any XI you like: exactly 11 slots, exactly one GK.

No board yet — pick a formation in the Formation tab and tap Confirm formation. Your board becomes a list of the 11 positions + 12 bench picks; tap any slot to fill it (1 player = committed, 2–5 = a hold, or mark it busted).

Slot

1 player = committed · 2–5 = a hold · busted = ignored. Bench picks count too — the solver builds the best XI across everything.

Board

Change formation

⚠ Your card pool and hold groups are preserved, but slot assignments will be stale — the board will show everything as pending. Review your board after switching to make sure each pick is in the right slot before you solve.
Advanced — solver settings
7590105

The highest team rating the solver assumes is reachable — a speed lever, and the biggest one there is. With Optimal RC on (the default) the solver computes the exact best ceiling for your draft and sets it before every solve — no guessing, no warnings. Turn it off to drive the slider yourself: a lower ceiling searches far fewer nodes but can miss a better squad, a higher one guarantees completeness but is slower. Left on, you always get the fastest ceiling that’s still provably optimal.

Past your device’s core count this stops helping and some devices won’t handle the top end well.

Search budget for the main starting-XI solve, before it reports a capped result and offers a deeper search (x3 escalation). Default 5M.

Search budget for the inline pick helper (the “check this pick” button while drafting), before it reports a capped result and offers a deeper search. Default 3M.

Only search for squads reaching this final score (incl. manager +2). 0 = off.

0% (all shuffle)50 / 50100% (all divstatic)

Splits the same node cap into phases: a %divstatic share first (best-first, establishes a strong incumbent), then that many even, independently-seeded shuffle passes exploring different regions of the tree — keeping whichever phase scored highest. Any phase that finishes its whole tree still returns the proven optimum. Note: this can occasionally score below 0 shuffle passes at the same budget — shrinking %divstatic isn't free, since divstatic itself sometimes still needs its later budget to improve, not just to prove it's already optimal. Tested sweet spot: 50–75% divstatic with 8–16 shuffle passes; low %divstatic (≤25%) tested as a net loss regardless of pass count, and gains from more passes taper off past ~16–24.

Controls only how hard the bench picks are searched — it never changes your starting XI. Higher tiers add confidence, rarely a different answer. Very Slow can be extremely slow on complex drafts for little benefit.

Per-tie search budget for bench picks, before the solver reports a capped result and offers to keep searching. Default 3M.

0 = no tie analysis, 1 = check all ties; in between scales how many are checked.

Per-tie search budget for manager (+2/+1) analysis, before the solver reports a capped result and offers to keep searching. Default 3M.

Manager

Search a manager by name — its nation + league are what score (+2 when both are fresh vs your XI). The solver already breaks XI ties to keep the most managers usable. Manage the manager database (import / export / reset / add) in the DB tab.
Not in the list? Add manually

The DB isn't exhaustive — type any nation + league (Icons included) and add it. Works exactly the same in the solver. Tap any manager below to edit it here too.

Reset

Clears all picks, managers, the current solve, and resets the formation selection.

Save / log

Updates live as you build. Download saves your whole pool + formation + managers (and last solve). Import restores it.
The final draft score is the mathematically proven maximum once a solve finishes uncapped — an incorrect result can only come from a DB or UI error. If something looks wrong and you can't tell what was missed, download the .txt and report it.
These tune the in-pick helper. Each control says what it does, its default and its range — bump what you care about, reset any time.

Helper weights

Objective weights — calibrated from your DB

Runs a simulation over your currently loaded database to compute, from data, what each pick-time factor is actually worth — then ranks options with those weights instead of the manual ones above. Deterministic (same DB + seed → same result). Takes a few seconds.

Bump the things you care about; reset any time.

Advanced — rarity curves & diminishing

Fine controls for rarity curves, clash penalties and over-representation. Grouped by what they affect; each shows its default and range. Changes save automatically.

Icon
Hero
Prem-hero
Normal
Clash & redundancy
Common overrepresentation
Misc
Pure card evaluator (no board context)

Scores cards in isolation using only the weights above — no board, no clash penalties, no formation context. Useful for comparing raw card value before a draft or away from the board.

Formation weights
⚠ Proof of concept — primary positions are not yet reliable. First-listed position is used as primary; for multi-position cards this is often wrong. Formation ranking below should be treated as inaccurate until real primary data is added.
Objective formation ranking

Ranks formations by which positions hold the best players. Uses your calibrated card weights (Helper tab); auto-fits a quick default if you haven’t calibrated those yet. While calibrated, this replaces the manual weights below; Clear restores them.

Tap ± to flip the sign — negative values make the position unwanted
Pivot analyzer (v16 prototype)

Ranks every viable league and nation pivot by best-XI ceiling and copies-weighted supply. Advisory only — never constrains the solver. Top 5 shown; remainder collapsed below.

Player database

—

Manager database

—

Remove / edit a card

Search for a card — tap a result to edit its fields inline, or tap × to delete it.

Remove / edit a manager

Search the manager database — tap a result to edit its fields inline, or tap × to delete it.

Add a card to the database

Adds one card to the database so it shows up in name search everywhere. It persists in this browser automatically — export if you want a backup or want to carry it to another browser/device.

Add a manager to the database

Adds one manager to the database so it shows up in the manager search. It persists in this browser automatically — export if you want a backup or want to carry it to another browser/device.
